hooks.text: You fight your supplier for a two percent discount, then hand your bank four percent on the same payment without blinking. One is loud. One is silent.
There is a number on every supplier payment your bank hopes you never add up for a full year. Once a business owner sees it, the wire never goes the same way again.
Four percent of everything you send to suppliers this year never reaches them. On half a million in sourcing, that is twenty thousand dollars, gone.
A business owner closed a hard year, proud of every margin won. Then the accountant circled one line: twenty thousand dollars lost to bank FX, and not a cent of it coming back.
That four percent your bank skims off each supplier payment is not a fee you can dispute later. It is gone the second the wire clears, every year, forever.
